posts.text:
The Energy Department is delivering on President Trump’s Agenda to Reinvigorate the Nuclear Industrial Base:

Today, @DOE_EDF announced financing that will help rebuild America’s nuclear supply chain and accelerate the deployment of 10 new large-scale reactors. https://t.co/1IOyftvUHq
posts.summary: The US Department of Energy announced financing to rebuild America's nuclear supply chain and deploy 10 new large-scale reactors, which could have minimal direct implications for oil markets since nuclear energy primarily competes with natural gas and coal for electricity generation rather than petroleum products.

posts.text: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is “fully” open to commercial shipping and that large volumes of oil have been transported through the waterway in recent days, according to the semi-official Iranian Students’ News Agency.
posts.summary: Iran claims the Strait of Hormuz remains fully operational with large oil volumes passing through recently, suggesting no disruption to one of the world's most critical oil transit routes. The statement indicates oil supply flows through the chokepoint are unimpeded, which could ease concerns about potential supply constraints.

posts.text:
Kpler's drone-based tank level data is sending a clear warning signal at cushing

The US crude market's most-watched inventory hub has shed around 11 million barrels since early May, and at the current draw pace, stocks are on course to test operational tank bottoms by mid-July. https://t.co/OHrryNIn62
posts.summary: Cushing crude inventories have declined by approximately 11 million barrels since early May, and if current withdrawal rates persist, stocks could reach operational minimums by mid-July, signaling potential supply tightness in the US crude market's primary storage hub.

posts.text: The Hormuz crisis is triggering a global race to build oil reserves. From India and Australia to Singapore and Pakistan, governments are expanding storage capacity to boost energy security, potentially creating demand for up to 1 billion barrels over the coming years. #Oil
posts.summary: Concerns about the Hormuz Strait are prompting multiple governments to increase their oil storage capacity, which could generate demand for up to 1 billion barrels globally over the coming years. This strategic reserve expansion reflects efforts by countries across Asia and beyond to strengthen their energy security.

posts.text: There are tolls for the usage of the  Suez and Panama canals..both are considered international waterways ...just sayin https://t.co/1qAgZyiccq
posts.summary: Tracy Shuchart notes that both the Suez and Panama canals charge tolls for usage despite being classified as international waterways, implying a precedent for toll collection on critical shipping routes.

posts.text:
Strait of Hormuz crossings rebound

Kpler’s rolling coverage of US-Iran diplomatic developments shows a clear shift in Strait of Hormuz activity across two consecutive weekends. Between 12–14 June, total crossings reached 32. One week later, from 19–21 June, crossings rose to 93 https://t.co/L03f6wFOx1
posts.summary: Strait of Hormuz crossings nearly tripled from 32 to 93 between two consecutive weekends in mid-to-late June, suggesting reduced tensions in US-Iran relations and potentially increased oil flows through this critical chokepoint.

posts.text: ALERT: #GasPrices appear to be rising to $3.99/gal in #Ohio today. This is a routine price cycle to an elevated amount due to the ongoing U.S./Iran situation.
posts.summary: Gas prices in Ohio are rising to approximately $3.99 per gallon due to a routine price cycle linked to the U.S./Iran geopolitical situation. The increase reflects oil market sensitivity to tensions in the Middle East.

posts.text: ALERT: #GasPrices appear to be rising to $3.89/gal in #Florida today. This is a routine price cycle to an elevated amount due to the ongoing U.S./Iran situation.
posts.summary: Gas prices in Florida are rising to $3.89 per gallon due to geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran, which is driving a routine price cycle upward in the oil market.

posts.text: The Strait of Hormuz may be reopening, but tanker markets remain in crisis mode. Daily tanker rates have nearly doubled to over $190,000, while some VLCCs are reportedly earning up to $470,000 per day as vessel shortages persist. #Oil #Tankers #Hormuz #Shipping
posts.summary: Despite potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates have surged dramatically—doubling to over $190,000 per day—due to vessel shortages, with some very large crude carriers earning up to $470,000 daily, signaling continued supply-side constraints in global oil shipping markets.

posts.text:

posts.summary: Rory Johnston discussed shipping behavior during the Hormuz crisis and the timeline for normalizing oil flows in the latest episode of his Oil Ground Up podcast. The episode explores the factors that influenced shipping decisions during this period of market disruption.

posts.text: BRENT futures for deliveries next year are falling as traders anticipate there will be plenty of oil available to meet consumer requirements and start refilling depleted inventories. posts.text:
#Commodities:

The Bloomberg Commodity Total Return Index is heading for its worst monthly performance since 2022, currently down around 10% in June and reducing its year-to-date gain to 15%. All sectors are trading lower this month, led by a 16% slump in energy and a 12% decline
posts.summary: Energy commodities, particularly oil, are experiencing significant declines in June with a 16% monthly slump, contributing to the Bloomberg Commodity Index's worst monthly performance since 2022. Despite year-to-date gains of 15%, the sharp June selloff across all commodity sectors signals weakening demand or market pressures affecting crude prices.

posts.text:
Approvals for new gas-fired power plants rose to a 25-year high last year

Until 2023, most gas-fired power plant approvals were in gas-importing countries, but the US now dominates orders, driven by data centre demand

Learn more in our report ➡️ https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/DBX0xeI2as
posts.summary: Gas-fired power plant approvals reached a 25-year high in 2023, with the US leading demand driven primarily by data center growth rather than traditional energy needs. This surge in natural gas infrastructure approvals could support sustained demand for natural gas, though direct oil market implications are not addressed in the provided text.

posts.text: UAE’s exit from OPEC+ reduced the group’s share of crude oil production and capacity. #TodayInEnergy https://t.co/n7FqyRemAh https://t.co/4c2K6dGU95
posts.summary: The UAE's departure from OPEC+ has decreased the organization's overall share of global crude oil production and capacity. This reduction weakens OPEC+'s market influence and control over oil supply dynamics.

posts.text:

posts.summary: The IEA warns that the Middle East conflict is causing a historic disruption in global oil supply, resulting in higher prices for consumers, and recommends ten demand-side measures to mitigate the economic impact.

posts.text:

posts.summary: US ethanol demand in 2026 may grow through improved blending economics and global energy security needs rather than regulatory mandates from Washington, potentially creating new market opportunities. The shift suggests ethanol could gain competitiveness based on market fundamentals and international factors rather than domestic policy support.

posts.text: Keep an eye on European refiners this week. They are NOT configured to operate when ambient temperatures are above 100 degrees Fahrenheit.  Lower yields are likely across the Continent.
posts.summary: European refiners face operational constraints during high ambient temperatures above 100°F, which could reduce fuel yields across the continent this week. This supply constraint may tighten European oil product markets and support prices for refined fuels.

posts.text:
We have more insight than ever. So why are decisions getting harder? 

Markets are more volatile. Decisions more complex. The cost of delay is higher than ever. 

The challenge isn’t knowing more. It’s acting, quickly and with clarity. 

CERA Titan: https://t.co/vhIqgYP6tU https://t.co/lt4iZvjVMw
posts.summary: S&P Global Energy highlights that despite increased market data availability, energy decision-makers face growing complexity and volatility, making rapid execution increasingly critical. The implication is that companies need tools to convert data insights into swift, decisive action in an unstable market environment.

posts.text: Open seasons are a key part of marketing capacity for gas pipeline and storage projects, but they can also raise several regulatory questions. And the answers depend on what kind of open season it is. Today, we take a deep dive into the ins and outs of a gas project open season. https://t.co/QCrBan0kkp
posts.summary: Open seasons are a marketing mechanism for gas pipeline and storage projects that involve regulatory considerations depending on their structure. While primarily relevant to natural gas infrastructure, such projects can have indirect implications for integrated energy markets and project development timelines.

posts.text: Gasoline retailers continue to be the quiet beneficiaries of margin expansion thanks to Iran War. Circle K parent (Couche-Tard) saw gross US margins of 52.44cts/gal in the US in the quarter ending April 26, 2026  - - up 9.17cts/gal from last year.
posts.summary: Gasoline retailers are experiencing expanded profit margins amid geopolitical tensions, with Circle K's US gross margins reaching 52.44 cents per gallon in Q2 2026, up 9.17 cents year-over-year. The margin expansion suggests retail fuel prices are rising faster than wholesale costs, benefiting convenience store operators during the period of elevated oil prices.
